fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alaska Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Alaska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity alaska, how to transfer dietitian license to alaska, dietitian compact alaska, alaska dietitian endorsement application

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: $100 nonrefundable application fee + $50 initial license fee
quick_facts.license_duration: Up to 2 Years (depending on issue date relative to the cycle)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: The credential required to practice dietetics in Alaska, overseen by the Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing. The state also separately licenses 'Nutritionists'.

steps.title: Create MyLicense Account
steps.description: Visit the MyLicense portal (https://www.commerce.alaska.gov/MyLicense) to register for a new account or log in with your MyAlaska credentials. This is the primary system for all professional licensing applications in Alaska.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Within the MyLicense portal, select the Board of Dietitians and Nutritionists and fill out the initial application form, providing all required personal, educational, and supervised practice experience details.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for official submission of all required documents, including official academic transcripts (e.g., bachelor's degree or higher in dietetics/nutrition), verification of 900 hours of supervised practice from an ACEND-accredited program, and proof of passing a national dietetic examination (e.g., CDR exam).

steps.title: Pay Application and Initial License Fees
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee ($50) and the prorated initial biennial license fee ($200 for a full cycle) through the MyLicense portal using a credit card.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: The Alaska Board of Dietitians and Nutritionists will review your completed application and all supporting documents. Once approved, your license will be issued and accessible through the MyLicense portal.

documents.detail: Application with notarized signature and a recent passport-style photograph.
Must be sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
If applying with existing licenses, verification from all other states must be submitted.

provisional_text: Alaska does not explicitly outline a temporary or provisional license for dietitians. You must complete the CDR exam and obtain full registration prior to licensure.
reciprocity_text: Alaska offers licensure by endorsement for those already licensed in another state. Applicants must demonstrate that their current state's requirements are substantially equivalent to Alaska's and must submit verifications for all current and past licenses.

faqs.question: How much does an Alaska dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is $150, which includes a $100 application fee and a $50 initial license fee.

faqs.question: When does the Alaska license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Alaska dietitian licenses renew biennially and expire on December 31st of odd-numbered years. The renewal period requires a $125 fee. There is no grace period for practicing on a lapsed license.

faqs.question: Does Alaska require a background check?
faqs.answer: While an extensive FBI fingerprint check is not centrally required for all applicants in this program, individuals must answer professional fitness questions and may need to provide court documents if they have a disciplinary history.

faqs.question: Does Alaska have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Alaska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Alaska offers licensure by endorsement for those already licensed in another state. Applicants must demonstrate that their current state's requirements are substantially equivalent to Alaska's and must submit verifications for all current and past licenses.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Alaska
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian and Nutritionist license in Alaska, users must complete the application process through the MyLicense portal, the state's online professional licensing system.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the MyLicense portal (https://www.commerce.alaska.gov/MyLicense), click 'New Account Registration' or 'Login' if you have a MyAlaska account, and follow the prompts to create an account or link existing credentials. Once logged in, navigate to 'Professional Licensing' and select the Board of Dietitians and Nutritionists to initiate your application.
